reasoning: The question asks whether at least three of Trump's cabinet nominees (including 15 department heads and 9 cabinet-level officials, totaling 24 initial positions) will be withdrawn or voted down by April 1, 2025. All tracking sources up to March 11 confirm that out of the 24 positions, the vast majority have already been successfully confirmed by the Senate. A March 11, 2025 article explicitly notes that Elise Stefanik's nomination for Ambassador to the UN is the ONLY Cabinet-level position left to be finalized. Since only one nominee (Gaetz) has failed so far, and arguably only one position is left (Stefanik), it is mathematically impossible to reach three failures by the deadline, even if Stefanik fails (which would make two failures). Another individual, Dave Weldon, was withdrawn for CDC Director, but the CDC Director is not a cabinet-level position specified in the resolution criteria. The Manifold market explicitly prices this bet at 1% for this reason. I will output 0.02 (the system floor) because reaching three failures is essentially impossible under the rules since 23 out of 24 original positions are locked in.
